jokes.text: Leslie's fashion show while Ben tries to prep her for interview
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to prepare for important interview
jokes.punchline: She's distracted by playing fashion show instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie prioritizing fun over work, character-driven humor with good integration

jokes.text: But I bet these pants will work for the public
jokes.setup: Ben asks about public works project
jokes.punchline: Leslie makes pun about pants 'working for the public'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Leslie's character, economical delivery

jokes.text: And I bet they look even better on Ben's floor
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about clothes on Ben's floor
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo but well-delivered and economical

jokes.text: Are you hitting on Leslie... for me?
jokes.setup: Ann makes sexual comment about Leslie
jokes.punchline: Ben confused if Ann is flirting on his behalf
jokes.explanation: Original concept with perfect Ben confusion and economic delivery

jokes.text: It says 'Nympho' on the butt in silver, sparkly letters
jokes.punchline: Reveal of inappropriate text on the butt
jokes.explanation: Strong visual reveal with excellent structure and timing

jokes.text: Ann's clinical explanation of 'nympho' and butt implications
jokes.punchline: Ann's overly clinical explanation of the implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ann character moment with clinical approach to inappropriate topic

jokes.text: They're all girls. Women's studies.
jokes.setup: Struggling to remember women's studies facts
jokes.punchline: Andy's obvious realization about women's studies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - obvious observation delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Usually tests scare me, but this is an oral exam. And if there's one thing I know is--
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates sexual innuendo about oral exam
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo but fits Andy's oblivious character

jokes.text: My fantastic-- it's talk. God, I don't know.
jokes.punchline: Catches himself and awkwardly corrects
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Andy catching his mistake

jokes.text: If you can't remember what some woman did, just tear up and say 'she changed my life'
jokes.punchline: April's cynical advice to fake emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect April cynicism with quotable, subversive advice

jokes.text: Maybe we should see if he can pull that rod out of his butt and we can hang some of those outfits on it
jokes.setup: Commenting on Ben being tightly wound
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor about using the rod as clothing rack
jokes.explanation: Creative visual metaphor that extends the basic insult effectively

jokes.text: We've gone 30 hours without breaking up. Our personal best is 47 hours.
jokes.setup: Andy and Ann are in a groove
jokes.punchline: Reveals their relationship success is measured in hours
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of relationship milestones with specific, absurd numbers

jokes.text: It happened when she was out of town for two days and she forgot we were dating
jokes.punchline: Reveals it only worked because Ann forgot they were together
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the relationship even more absurd

jokes.text: Come on, did anyone even get hurt?
jokes.setup: Interview cancelled due to factory explosion
jokes.punchline: Leslie's priorities are backwards about tragedy
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate - shows Leslie's single-minded focus

jokes.text: Do you know who the president of boring club is? Me? Nope... You lost the election 'cause your speech was too boring.
jokes.setup: Setting up who's president of boring club
jokes.punchline: Reveals Ben lost for being too boring for boring club
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect escalation of Ben's boringness

jokes.text: Andy's long pause before repeating the exact same fact
jokes.punchline: Andy just repeats memorized fact after thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of pause to show Andy has nothing beyond memorized facts

jokes.text: Just one man's opinion
jokes.punchline: Treating objective fact as personal opinion
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy logic treating facts as opinions

jokes.text: I was hoping for a P-plus, but that does not exist
jokes.punchline: Disappointed by non-existent better grade
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his misunderstanding of grading

jokes.text: Basically, best grade in the class-- tied for the best grade in the class
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself but still claims best grade
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's need to be the best even in pass/fail

jokes.text: We'll let you pay for your own food, because of equality
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands equality as making her pay
jokes.explanation: Clever misapplication of feminist principles, very Andy

jokes.text: I have a date with my bathtub, a glass of red wine, and a gigantic fireman named Marcus
jokes.punchline: Reveals Marcus is probably a romance novel character
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing Ann's romance novel habit

jokes.text: Maybe we should all just drink sensible portions of milk
jokes.punchline: Takes boring suggestion to extreme with milk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben over-caution taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: It's like dealing with a strict mother who I am confusingly attracted to
jokes.punchline: Admits to confusing maternal attraction
jokes.explanation: Funny relationship dynamic with uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: Ben is like a MILF
jokes.punchline: Applies MILF concept to Ben
jokes.explanation: Brilliant gender-swapped application of MILF, very quotable

jokes.text: I'm watching Murder She Wrote. Murder She Wrote. I'm watching Murder She Wrote.
jokes.setup: Leslie lying to Ben about being home
jokes.punchline: Nervous repetition reveals she's lying
jokes.explanation: Standard bad lying behavior, fits Leslie's character

jokes.text: We are so 'drucky.' I mean 'lunky.'
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'drunk' and 'lucky' into nonsense words
jokes.explanation: Good drunk wordplay that reveals Leslie's state

jokes.text: I still had eight hours rental time on that hot-tub limousine
jokes.punchline: Tom casually mentions having a hot-tub limo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom extravagance mentioned casually

jokes.text: Why are you from Russia?
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical question
jokes.explanation: Absurd question that shows they're all drunk

jokes.text: My father once told my mother that woman was made from the rib of Adam, and my mom broke his jaw
jokes.setup: Ron discussing powerful women in his life
jokes.punchline: His mother's violent response to sexism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron backstory with dark humor and strong female influence

jokes.text: I'm surrounded by friends... Friends I don't know yet
jokes.setup: Ron asking why April is eating alone
jokes.punchline: April's twisted logic about strangers being friends
jokes.explanation: Classic April anti-social logic delivered deadpan

jokes.text: It's the true story of a woman born with no arms and no legs who attempted to swim the English Channel... Oh, she drowned immediately
jokes.punchline: Reveals the obviously impossible outcome matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor with absurd premise

jokes.text: I don't know, Marcus. I'll hit you up when I hit you up.
jokes.setup: Ann canceling her romance novel bath
jokes.punchline: Talking to fictional character like real boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that develops the Marcus joke further

jokes.text: 'Helpy' to hop out
jokes.punchline: Creates nonsense word mixing 'happy' and 'help'
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't really work, feels forced

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. You're being serious.
jokes.setup: Leslie calling Pawnee the greatest city in the world
jokes.punchline: Buddy realizes she actually means it
jokes.explanation: Good outsider perspective on Leslie's Pawnee enthusiasm

jokes.text: Found a small piece of carpet without a horrifying smellscape
jokes.punchline: Airport is so bad they had to find non-smelly spot
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about Pawnee's sad airport

jokes.text: Pawnee, Indiana, home to the Sweetums Candy Corporation, 19 toxic-waste repositories, and not much else
jokes.punchline: Lists toxic waste as main feature
jokes.explanation: Good outsider's harsh but accurate assessment of Pawnee

jokes.text: friendly faces and hand-working hards-- hardworking hands
jokes.punchline: Mixes up words due to being drunk
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk speech mistake, fits situation

jokes.text: If they got together, they would make the most beautiful super baby. It would rule us all.
jokes.punchline: Imagines their offspring would dominate humanity
jokes.explanation: Good April observation with dark escalation

jokes.text: You have extraordinary caput laterale
jokes.punchline: Uses overly scientific term for basic compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris over-intellectualizing a simple compliment

jokes.text: Well, like I said, I don't want to discuss it, Buddy, and, frankly, I don't appreciate your condescending tone. But you know what? I've had a couple drinks, so what the hell?
jokes.setup: Leslie refusing to discuss her affair
jokes.punchline: Immediately changes mind because she's drunk
jokes.explanation: Good drunk logic moment where alcohol overrides judgment

jokes.text: Yes, when you have a jumbo Margarita and some flaming Tequila shots, you can get drunk
jokes.punchline: Leslie explains alcohol causes drunkenness
jokes.explanation: Obvious drunk logic but fits the situation

jokes.text: This-- this is not because I'm drunk. This is because of the wire.
jokes.punchline: Blames microphone wire instead of alcohol
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk denial behavior

jokes.text: The sheep that watch my show are gonna eat this up like tiramisu
jokes.setup: Buddy explaining why he'll air the tape
jokes.punchline: Mixes sheep metaphor with dessert
jokes.explanation: Decent mixed metaphor showing Buddy's pretentiousness

jokes.text: And sheep don't eat it
jokes.punchline: Leslie points out literal problem with metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good drunk literal thinking that catches the metaphor error

jokes.text: And I'm gonna lock down that P-plus
jokes.punchline: Still doesn't understand P-plus doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Andy learned nothing

jokes.text: You and the women taught me that nothing is impossible
jokes.punchline: Andy uses women's studies lessons to contradict her
jokes.explanation: Clever use of feminist empowerment for absurd goal

jokes.text: It ain't government work if you don't have to do it twice
jokes.punchline: Self-aware government inefficiency joke
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating government humor from Jerry

jokes.text: You'd think a commuter airport could handle the luggage of 11 people at once, but apparently not
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about tiny airport's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Standard small-town incompetence humor

jokes.text: How much is a Walkman nowadays? Probably more than $20. Here's $25.
jokes.setup: Ron offering money to young people
jokes.punchline: Completely out of touch with technology and prices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron out-of-touch moment with obsolete technology

jokes.text: Great red shirt
jokes.setup: Tension after Ron's confession about Linda
jokes.punchline: Chris awkwardly compliments Ron's shirt to avoid topic
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Chris moment deflecting with random compliment

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Ron... It was you who told me that. That's crazy.
jokes.setup: Andy using Ron's advice against him
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's quoting Ron to Ron
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment with Andy realizing the irony

jokes.text: I'm quite lonely
jokes.setup: Chris trying to be gracious about Linda situation
jokes.punchline: Sudden honest admission of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris vulnerable moment breaking his positive facade

jokes.text: We have now gone 48 hours without breaking up, shattering our previous record
jokes.punchline: Callbacks to their terrible relationship record
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows slight improvement in their dysfunction

jokes.text: 48 of 'em. Different flavor for each hour.
jokes.setup: Andy giving Ann condoms as anniversary gift
jokes.punchline: Specific mathematical reasoning for gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic applying math to inappropriate gift

jokes.text: Yeah, we're not dating any more, okay? Get out.
jokes.punchline: Ann immediately ends relationship
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to their relationship dysfunction with swift ending
